title: Repeated UI error messages when Policies are not correct
body: When a ClusterImagePolicy or ImagePolicy CRs don't present the right keyData, same error message is shown repeatedly on UI. Some examples below: `Error: checking signature of "quay.io/rh-ee-anahas/imagedigest@sha256:531eaae43197a403f9ae14b900480ba8d7a4b4893153072beee79e8572fa1af0": verifying signatures: SignatureValidationFailed: None of the signatures were accepted, reasons: parsing public key 1: asn1: structure error: tags don't match (3 vs {class:0 tag:0 length:2 isCompound:false}) {optional:false explicit:false application:false private:false defaultValue: tag: stringType:0 timeType:0 set:false omitEmpty:false} BitString @23; parsing public key 1: asn1: structure error: tags don't match (3 vs {class:0 tag:0 length:2 isCompound:false}) {optional:false explicit:false application:false private:false defaultValue: tag: stringType:0 timeType:0 set:false omitEmpty:false} BitString @23; parsing public key 1: asn1: structure error: tags don't match (3 vs {class:0 tag:0 length:2 isCompound:false}) {optional:false explicit:false application:false private:false defaultValue: tag: stringType:0 timeType:0 set:false omitEmpty:false} BitString @23` Log attached: [error.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21413968/error.txt) Other examples: `Failed to pull image "quay.io/rh-ee-anahas/testsignedimage@sha256:b72960b93a140be0c394b023259124d452564dbb7ab11ba37039314f1ecc7052": SignatureValidationFailed: Source image rejected: None of the signatures were accepted, reasons: cryptographic signature verification failed: invalid signature when validating ASN.1 encoded signature; cryptographic signature verification failed: invalid signature when validating ASN.1 encoded signature; cryptographic signature verification failed: invalid signature when validating ASN.1 encoded signature` `Failed to pull image "quay.io/rh-ee-anahas/testsignedimage@sha256:b72960b93a140be0c394b023259124d452564dbb7ab11ba37039314f1ecc7052": SignatureValidationFailed: Source image rejected: None of the signatures were accepted, reasons: parsing public key 1: x509: unsupported elliptic curve; parsing public key 1: x509: unsupported elliptic curve; parsing public key 1: x509: unsupported elliptic curve`

comments.body:
Thanks for your report.

Some of that is unavoidable — if there are 3 signatures and they don’t validate, well, they don’t validate, and that needs to be reported one by one.

But in the cases where loading / setting up the public key fails, yes, it should be possible (and be a bit more efficient) to only set up the public key once (note to self: in `isRunningImageAllowed`; sharing code with, but not calling the existing `isSignatureAuthorAccepted`). Or, perhaps, the keys could be set up at process startup time, but in that case an invalid public key would prevent using images from all other repos as well, and it would have to be fixed immediately.
